name: elastic
explanation: The check only requires a URL (default `http://localhost:9200`) — Elasticsearch's REST API is on a well-known port. However, virtually all production deployments enable xpack security and require basic auth and TLS, so a discovery layer must (a) probe the port for the ES root document, (b) detect whether security is enabled, and (c) defer to the existing auto_conf template for the credentialed path. Classification is custom because of the deployment-mode branching even though the unauthenticated case is trivial.
notes: Cloud-managed clusters (Elastic Cloud, AWS OpenSearch) always need credentials → fall through to credentials-required in practice.
